name: Cross-Platform Content Calendar Skill
description: Governed 5-step calendar dispatch skill for SteeleZone and DiscoBass. Reads config/post_schedule.csv, generates one dispatch proposal per scheduled row for the target date, audits all proposals, writes validated proposals to manifests/staging/ for operator review, and reports. No platform post fires automatically — operator promotes proposals. Three human approval gates apply.

schedule_date.description: Target date to dispatch in YYYY-MM-DD format. Must match rows in config/post_schedule.csv.

brand_filter.description: Brand to dispatch for. Enum: steelezone | discobass | all. Default: all.

trigger_reason.description: Why this calendar run is being triggered. Non-empty; logged for audit trail.

proposals_path.description: Path to the staged dispatch proposal file written to manifests/staging/.

audit_verdict.description: pass or halt — audit agent decision across all proposals.

report_path.description: Path to the operator-review summary written to reports/.

steps.name: Validate schedule file schema
steps.description: Repo Agent reads config/post_schedule.csv and validates: file exists, required columns present (post_id, video_id, platform, brand, scheduled_date, time_slot, caption_template_id, hashtag_set_id, link_url), brand column contains only approved values (steelezone | discobass), no PII patterns in any cell. Halts if schedule is missing, malformed, or contains unapproved brand values.

safety.description: Pure read-only schedule validation. Any schema violation, PII, or unapproved brand value halts the run immediately.

steps.name: Generate dispatch proposals from schedule
steps.description: Workflow Agent reads config/post_schedule.csv rows matching schedule_date and brand_filter. For each matching row, generates a structured dispatch proposal: resolves the logical n8n endpoint name from platform+brand mapping, builds payload from schedule columns, attaches trigger_reason and n8n_run_id. No HTTP calls. Proposals are held in memory until step 3 audit passes.

safety.description: Read-only proposal generation. No network calls, no file writes. Any row referencing an unknown endpoint or forbidden platform halts the run.

steps.name: Audit all proposals for violations
steps.description: Audit Agent validates every proposal generated in step 2: category in {content_automation}, no forbidden platforms or categories, all payload keys within the endpoint's allowed_payload_keys list, no PII patterns in payload values, auth env var is a name not a value. Any proposal violation halts the entire run — partial dispatch is not permitted.

safety.description: Audit only. Writes audit verdict to logs/ and reports/. Any single proposal violation halts the entire run — all-or-nothing enforcement.

steps.name: Write validated proposals to manifests/staging/
steps.description: Manifest Agent writes the full set of audit-approved proposals to manifests/staging/calendar_proposals_<date>.json. Each proposal includes: endpoint logical name, payload, trigger_reason, audit_verdict=pass, CR reference. The proposal file is not executable — it is a human-reviewable record that the operator promotes to trigger actual dispatch.

safety.description: Writes proposals to manifests/staging/ only. Proposals cannot self-promote — operator must explicitly trigger production dispatch after reviewing this file.

steps.name: Report: write operator-review summary
steps.description: Reporting Agent writes a human-readable summary to reports/: schedule_date, brand_filter, proposal_count per brand and platform, audit verdict, path to staged proposals file, and next-step instructions (which human approval gates apply, how to promote). Never logs full URLs, tokens, or PII.

safety.description: Writes only to logs/workflows/ and reports/. Summary is the operator's primary artifact for review before approving dispatch.
